title: daucus carota subsp. parviflorus
intro: Daucus carota subsp. parviflorus, also known as wild carrot or small-flowered wild carrot, is a subspecies of the wild carrot plant, characterized by its smaller flowers and more delicate appearance compared to the common carrot. The medicinal properties of Daucus carota subsp. parviflorus include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Daucus carota subsp. parviflorus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications, such as salves and creams, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
